commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

learningObjectives.text: Compare ui versus remote state using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain server-state lifecycle using a new example and justify the result.

learningObjectives.subtopic: Caching and synchronization with React Query
learningObjectives.text: Explain caching and synchronization with react query using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **UI versus remote state** → **Server-state lifecycle** → **Caching and synchronization with React Query**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: UI versus remote state is best understood as follows: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.
choices.label: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of UI versus remote state.
choices.label: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of UI versus remote state.
choices.label: Place state in the lowest component that owns all consumers and updates. Move it upward or into a shared mechanism only when ownership demands it.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of UI versus remote state.
choices.label: Use state for changing information that affects rendering, ordinary variables for render calculations, and refs for mutable non-rendered values.

questions.explanation: UI versus remote state is best understood as follows: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.

choices.feedback: Server-state lifecycle is best understood as follows: Remote state moves through loading, error, stale, fresh, inactive, and updating states and can change outside the current client.

sections.body: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.
sections.title: UI versus remote state

sections.body: Remote state moves through loading, error, stale, fresh, inactive, and updating states and can change outside the current client.
sections.title: Server-state lifecycle

sections.body: TanStack Query identifies remote data by query keys, caches results, tracks freshness, refetches, and coordinates mutation invalidation.

summary: Modeling Application State and React Query connects UI versus remote state, Server-state lifecycle, Caching and synchronization with React Query.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Modeling Application State and React Query

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns modeling application state and react query from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Professional React Workflows and Ecosystem** module.
